pi_items.source: Task description explicitly states 'Set the Date to August 15, 2025'
Task description required notes text contains 'Hive #04'
Task description required notes text contains 'Alcohol wash test for Varroa destructor'
Task description required notes text contains 'Infestation level is at treatment threshold'

Task description specifies Quantity 1: Value '9'. Scientifically accurate: alcohol wash test treatment threshold is 1-3% infestation, which equals 3-9 mites in a 300-bee sample (Betterbee, PSU Extension, USDA ARS sources)
Task description specifies Quantity 1 Label 'Mite Count'
Task description specifies Quantity 2: Value '300'. Scientifically accurate: 300 bees is the standard sample size for alcohol wash testing (Betterbee, PSU Extension, USDA ARS, UFL IFAS sources)
Task description specifies Quantity 2 Label 'Sample Size'

Task description specifies Quantity 3: Value '3'. Verified: 9 mites / 300 bees × 100 = 3%. Scientifically accurate: 3% is at the economic treatment threshold per UFL IFAS, NC State, Iowa State, and Ontario Bee sources
Task description specifies Quantity 3 Label 'Infestation Rate'

privileged_info_summary: The task requires an AI agent to create a synthetic Observation log in the farmOS Field Kit app for recording an apiary mite wash inspection using the alcohol wash method to monitor Varroa destructor mites. The expected values are: Date 2025-08-15; Notes containing 'Hive #04', 'Alcohol wash', and 'treatment threshold'; and three quantities: Mite Count of 9 mites (value at high end of 1-3% treatment threshold range for 300-bee samples), Sample Size of 300 bees (standard alcohol wash test sample size), and Infestation Rate of 3% (mathematically: 9/300×100, equivalent to the economic treatment threshold). All metadata values are internally consistent, mathematically correct, and align with established beekeeping standards for Varroa monitoring. Data is synthetic and created by the agent via UI interaction.
